sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Form FWP BANK OF NOVA SCOTIA Filed by: BANK OF NOVA SCOTIA
feed.summary: The Bank of Nova Scotia has filed a Form FWP for a Senior Note Program, Series A, offering Equity Linked Securities – Market Linked Securities – Auto-Callable with Contingent Coupon with Memory Feature and Contingent Downside Principal at Risk Securities due February 23, 2029. These securities are linked to the performance of Amazon.com, Inc., Microsoft Corporation, and Netflix, Inc. The filing details the terms, including contingent coupon payments, automatic call features, maturity payment amounts, and various risks associated with the investment, such as the potential for loss of principal and credit risk of the Bank.

feed.title: Citius inks EU distribution agreement for CTCL treatment
feed.summary: Citius Oncology, a subsidiary of Citius Pharmaceuticals, has signed an exclusive distribution agreement with Unipha to make Lymphir (denileukin diftitox-cxdl) available outside the USA. This marks Citius Oncology's third international distribution partnership, expanding global access for their targeted immune therapy for relapsed or refractory cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL). Lymphir is intended for Stage I-III CTCL cases after at least one prior systemic therapy.

feed.title: Mirum Pharmaceuticals Reports Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
feed.summary: Mirum Pharmaceuticals announced inducement awards granted to 37 new employees, comprising stock options, restricted stock units (RSUs), and performance stock units (PSUs). These awards, approved by the Compensation Committee, are designed to align with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) and vest over several years based on continued service and, for PSUs, on achieving specific net product sales targets in 2027 and 2028. This move aims to incentivize and retain key talent within the company.

feed.title: Wabtec (NYSE:WAB) Price Target Raised to $290.00 at Stephens
feed.summary: Stephens has raised its price target for Wabtec (NYSE:WAB) to $290.00 from $230.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating, which suggests a potential upside of 14.2% for the transportation company's stock. This adjustment follows Wabtec's strong Q4 performance, exceeding EPS and revenue expectations, and its announcement of a record backlog of approximately $27 billion. The company also approved a $1.2 billion share buyback program and completed the acquisition of Dellner Couplers, further signaling strategic capital deployment and confidence in future growth.

feed.title: Occidental Refocuses On Upstream Efficiency And Debt After OxyChem Sale
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum has sold its OxyChem chemicals business to Berkshire Hathaway, intending to use the proceeds for debt reduction. The company has also restructured a Delaware Basin contract to a fixed-fee model to cut costs and enhance flexibility. These actions mark a strategic shift for Occidental Petroleum towards becoming a more focused energy company, prioritizing balance sheet strength, cash flow resilience, and debt reduction amidst fluctuating commodity prices.

feed.title: Does Stryker’s New Ortho-Robotics Launch and Results Reset the Bull Case For SYK?
feed.summary: Stryker recently launched the T2 Alpha Humerus Nailing System and began a limited market release of its Mako RPS handheld robotic system for total knee procedures. These new product offerings, along with reported full-year 2025 sales of US$25.12 billion and net income of US$3.25 billion, aim to strengthen Stryker's orthopedic and robotics ecosystem. The Mako RPS handheld system is particularly noted for potentially broadening the use cases for robotic-assisted knee procedures and reinforcing the company's investment narrative in orthopedic robotics growth, despite ongoing risks like supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: Hercules Capital Weighs Added Payouts Against New US300 Million Debt
feed.summary: Hercules Capital (NYSE:HTGC) has announced a new supplemental cash distribution to be paid over four quarters, in addition to its regular dividend. The company also completed a US$300 million unsecured notes offering to support its lending activities to growth companies. These actions raise questions for investors regarding income reliability, balance sheet flexibility, and the deployment of the newly acquired capital.

feed.title: MDGL Strengthens MASH Franchise With New Genetic Approaches
feed.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) has expanded its metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) pipeline through an exclusive global licensing agreement with Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co. Ltd., acquiring rights to six preclinical siRNA programs for an upfront payment of $60 million and potential milestone payments up to $4.4 billion. This move aims to develop innovative combination therapies with Madrigal's approved drug Rezdiffra, the first MASH treatment, and other pipeline assets to enhance treatment outcomes for the serious liver disease. Madrigal plans to submit investigational new drug applications for the initial siRNA candidates in 2026 and evaluate them in combination with Rezdiffra.

feed.title: Fortive Corp (FTV) Shares Down 3.37% on Feb 12
feed.summary: Fortive Corp (FTV) shares fell 3.37% in mid-day trading on February 12, reaching an intraday low of $56.68 before settling at $56.73. This places FTV 9.62% below its 52-week high. Wall Street analysts predict an average target price of $60.18, suggesting a 6.09% upside, while GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $42.39, indicating a potential downside of 25.28%.

feed.title: McDonald's Stock Hits New All-Time Highs And Has More Upside: Analyst Says 'Value Strategy Is Clearly Working'
feed.summary: McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) stock has reached new all-time highs, with analysts from TD Cowen and BTIG offering positive outlooks after strong fourth-quarter results. Both firms highlighted better-than-expected same-store sales growth and the success of McDonald's value strategy in attracting lower-income consumers, leading BTIG to raise its price target for the company. Despite some analyst caution regarding higher interest and CapEx guidance, the overall sentiment points to continued upside for MCD.

feed.title: T-Mobile Us Inc (NASDAQ:TMUS) Beats Market’s Expectations
feed.summary: T-Mobile Us Inc (NASDAQ:TMUS) reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with both revenue and earnings per share surpassing market expectations. The company saw a 4.87% stock increase and achieved significant postpaid customer growth, driven by its multi-layer 5G approach. Despite a decline in net income year-over-year due to higher operating expenses, T-Mobile demonstrated robust cash flow from operations and service revenue growth.

feed.title: Lincoln Electric (NASDAQ:LECO) Reaches New 1-Year High Following Strong Earnings
feed.summary: Lincoln Electric Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:LECO) reached a new 52-week high after reporting stronger-than-expected earnings, with the company's EPS topping analysts' estimates by $0.13. The industrial products company saw its revenue increase by 5.5% compared to the previous year. Analyst ratings are mixed but lean towards a "Moderate Buy," and institutional investors have significantly increased their holdings in LECO.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley CEO received a 32% raise in 2025
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley CEO Ted Pick received a substantial 32% raise in 2025, bringing his compensation package to $45 million, following the company's strongest year ever in terms of earnings per share. Other tech and finance CEOs, including Satya Nadella, Jensen Huang, and Jamie Dimon, also saw significant pay increases, though Elon Musk's potential Tesla compensation could dwarf them all. Pick's bonus is largely deferred in equity, linking his future earnings to the stock's performance.

feed.title: ResMed Tests Smart CPAP Pressure Tool in New Sleep Apnea Trial
feed.summary: ResMed (RMD) is conducting a new clinical trial to test a software tool, the APAP Pressure Match tool, designed to improve CPAP pressure settings for sleep apnea patients. This tool uses personalized data to fine-tune pressure ranges, aiming to be as effective as standard methods while simplifying treatment setup for clinicians. The trial, currently in the setup phase and not yet recruiting, signifies ResMed's focus on software-driven personalization in sleep and respiratory care, which could enhance adoption and pricing power if successful.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages Southwest Airlines Co. Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Discuss Their Rights
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating whether officers and directors of Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE: LUV) breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages long-term shareholders to contact them to discuss legal options, which may include seeking corporate governance reforms, return of funds, or financial incentives. Halper Sadeh LLC handles these actions on a contingent fee basis, aiming to improve company policies and recover funds for defrauded investors.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Waste Management (NYSE:WM) CAO Sells 1,021 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Waste Management's Chief Accounting Officer, John Carroll, sold 1,021 shares of the company's stock for over $239,000, reducing his stake by nearly 11%. This transaction occurred after the company reported quarterly earnings that missed analyst estimates. Despite the insider selling and earnings miss, the stock holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, and institutional investors have significantly increased their positions in the company.

feed.title: Mobile-health Network Solutions Expands 2026 Equity Incentive Plan to Include Class B Shares
feed.summary: Mobile-health Network Solutions (MNDR) updated its 2025 employee incentive framework on February 6, 2026, creating a 2026 Employee Incentive Plan that now includes Class B ordinary shares. This change, capping annual equity awards at 15% of total outstanding shares, aims to better align talent and shareholder interests and strengthen retention. The company's stock currently holds a "Sell" rating with a $1.00 price target from analysts, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates MNDR as "Neutral" due to financial performance challenges despite strong revenue growth.

feed.title: Joseph Wolk Purchases 400 Shares of Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) Stock
feed.summary: Joseph Wolk, a director at Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU), purchased 400 shares of the company's stock on February 11th for $42,128. This transaction follows Prudential raising its quarterly dividend to $1.40 per share, resulting in an annualized dividend of $5.60 and a yield of approximately 5.4%. Despite a slight miss on Q4 EPS, analysts currently rate PRU as a "Hold" with an average price target of $117.11.

feed.title: Marcus & Millichap stock hits 52-week low at $24.93
feed.summary: Marcus & Millichap's (MMI) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $24.93, marking a 32.65% decline over the past year due to challenging market conditions. Despite not being profitable in the last twelve months, the company maintains financial stability with an Altman Z-Score of 4.85. Investors are awaiting upcoming earnings on February 13, and the company's IPA Capital Markets division recently arranged a significant refinancing deal for a luxury multifamily property.

feed.title: (IE) Strategic Equity Report (IE:CA)
feed.summary: This strategic equity report for Ivanhoe Electric Inc. (IE:CA) provides trading plans with specific buy and short targets and stop losses. The report also includes AI-generated ratings for near, mid, and long terms, indicating "Weak" for near and mid, and "Strong" for long term. It also lists recent articles related to IE:CA, offering further insights into the company's performance and trading signals.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Publishes 2026 Market Trends Report
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. has released its 2026 Market Trends report, which offers a comprehensive analysis of the evolving insurance market. The report suggests that current rate conditions favor buyers who are well-prepared and have positive claims histories, while highlighting sectors where rates will remain firm. It also integrates insights from the 2025 acquisition of Risk Strategies, providing a deeper perspective on commercial coverage, employee benefits, and personal insurance.

feed.title: Intercont (Cayman) Limited 2026 Strategic Plan Release - Anchoring the Core Shipping Business, Multi-Sector Synergy Opens New Growth Trajectories
feed.summary: Intercont (Cayman) Limited released its 2026 strategic plan, focusing on anchoring its core shipping business while expanding into high-potential sectors. The company plans to grow its green ro-ro vessel shipping business, acquire the Web3 enterprise zCloak, and enter the Artificial Intelligence (AI) field. This strategy aims to create a "Green Shipping + Web3 & AI Infrastructure" growth matrix, leveraging Turkey as a hub for AI infrastructure deployment to build a platform with long-term growth characteristics.

feed.title: Is Costco Stock a Buy Now or Still Too Expensive to Touch?
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) is currently considered expensive based on its forward P/E multiple compared to industry averages and competitors, despite being slightly below its historical median P/E. Fundamentals remain strong due to its resilient membership model, consistent comparable sales growth, and expanding e-commerce capabilities. However, due to its premium valuation, Zacks Investment Research recommends a "Hold" for existing investors and suggests potential investors await a more attractive entry point, while its earnings estimates have been raised by analysts.

feed.title: Baird, Wells Fargo cut Inspire Medical on reimbursement uncertainty and weaker 20
feed.summary: Baird and Wells Fargo have downgraded Inspire Medical Systems due to uncertainties surrounding physician reimbursement changes and a lowered 2026 growth outlook. The coding modifications for Inspire V could reduce physician payments by 10% to 50%, impacting future utilization and new account growth. Despite beating Q4 revenue expectations, the company's revised outlook and potential for reduced procedure volumes led to the downgrades.

feed.title: Alliant Energy (LNT) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: What to Know Ahead of Q4 Release
feed.summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) is anticipated to surpass its Q4 earnings estimates for the quarter ended December 2025, despite an expected year-over-year decline in earnings and revenues. This optimism stems from a positive Earnings ESP of +0.58% combined with a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), suggesting a high probability of an earnings beat according to Zacks' research model. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond the earnings report for a comprehensive decision.

feed.title: Jakks Pacific (JAKK) May Report Negative Earnings: Know the Trend Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Jakks Pacific (JAKK) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. Despite a positive Earnings ESP of +46.55%, the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #5, making it difficult to definitively predict an earnings beat. Investors are advised to consider other factors alongside the company's Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. With a Zacks Earnings ESP of +0.25% and a Zacks Rank of #3, the company is highly likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate of $0.84 per share. While other factors influence stock movement, ED appears to be a compelling candidate for an earnings beat.

feed.title: FICO stock hits 52-week low at 1284.03 USD
feed.summary: Fair Isaac and Company Inc. (FICO) stock has reached a new 52-week low at $1284.03, reflecting a 28.43% decline over the past year, despite the company's strong gross profit margins and profitability. InvestingPro analysis suggests FICO is slightly undervalued with a "GOOD" financial health rating, even though it trades at a high P/E ratio.

feed.title: Southern Co. (SO) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Southern Co. (SO) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with analysts forecasting $0.56 EPS and $6.86 billion in revenue. However, a negative Earnings ESP of -4.76% and a Zacks Rank of #4 make it difficult to confidently predict an earnings beat. Investors are advised to consider other factors despite the company's history of beating EPS estimates in three of the last four quarters.

feed.title: Pool Corp. (POOL) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: Pool Corp. (POOL) is anticipated to announce earnings next week, with analysts expecting year-over-year growth in earnings per share and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's Most Accurate Estimate is higher than the Zacks Consensus Estimate, resulting in a positive Earnings ESP of +1.01%, which, combined with a Zacks Rank of #3, suggests a likely earnings beat. The report, due February 19, could influence the stock price depending on how actual results compare to expectations and management's subsequent business outlook.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: Live Nation (LYV) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
feed.summary: Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in Q4 earnings, with a loss of $1.01 per share, despite a projected 6.9% increase in revenues to $6.08 billion for the quarter ended December 2025. The Zacks Earnings ESP is -12.45% and the stock holds a Zacks Rank #5, making it difficult to predict an earnings beat. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond just an earnings beat or miss.

feed.title: January 2026 Is Strongest Booking Month in Regent Seven Seas Cruises History
feed.summary: Regent Seven Seas Cruises experienced its strongest booking month in history this January, with a 20% increase in bookings compared to January 2025. This record performance was driven by high demand for global destinations, particularly Pacific and South America itineraries, and a trend towards longer voyages and multiple cruise bookings. The success was aided by the "Upgrade Your Horizon Wave Season" offer.

feed.title: IBM’s Strategic Acquisition: A $11 Billion Bet on Real-Time Data for AI
feed.summary: IBM has announced its intention to acquire the data-streaming platform Confluent for approximately $11 billion in an all-cash transaction. This acquisition aims to enhance IBM's AI and hybrid cloud capabilities by integrating Confluent's real-time data streaming technology, which is built on Apache Kafka, to support advanced AI applications. The deal is expected to finalize by mid-2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals, and aligns with IBM's broader strategy to embed AI across its offerings, including new FlashSystem storage solutions.

feed.title: Xcel Stock in 2026: What the Numbers Say After 15% Returns Last Year
feed.summary: Xcel Energy in 2026 is projected to see continued growth, driven by an ambitious expansion of data center capacity from 3 GW to 6 GW by 2027 and a significant $60 billion capital investment plan. Despite these growth catalysts and past positive returns, a TIKR valuation model suggests a "Sell" recommendation, as the estimated 8.2% annualized return on Xcel stock falls short of a 10% equity hurdle rate, indicating potential for capital preservation rather than substantial risk-adjusted appreciation. The company's future performance hinges on successful execution of its infrastructure projects, regulatory approvals, and disciplined cost management.

feed.title: Baxter stock dives on Q4 EPS miss, company introduces new operating model
feed.summary: Baxter's stock fell after the company reported Q4 earnings that missed analyst expectations, despite sales exceeding forecasts. The company posted a significant loss and announced a new operating model designed to simplify the organization, accelerate innovation, and improve performance. CEO Andrew Hider expressed confidence in Baxter's long-term trajectory despite the challenging quarter.

feed.title: Is Costco Stock a Buy Now or Still Too Expensive to Touch?
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation is trading at a premium valuation with a forward 12-month P/E of 46.21, significantly higher than industry and S&P 500 averages, making it an expensive stock despite strong fundamentals. While its membership model and consistent sales growth provide resilience, analysts suggest a "Hold" rating, advising existing investors to maintain positions and new investors to await a more attractive entry point. The company continues to show robust performance, outperforming some peers year-to-date, but its high valuation warrants caution.

feed.title: American Electric Power: Ideally Positioned to Benefit From Growing Demand
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is seen as ideally positioned to benefit from growing demand, with its business strategy focused on regulated investments across numerous utilities. This strategy is expected to support management's 7% to 9% earnings growth target for 2026-2030, which analysts view as achievable. Recent developments in the PJM market are also considered positive for regulated transmission and distribution utilities like AEP.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) is expected to report an increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an anticipated EPS of $0.46, up 15% year-over-year, and revenues of $2.33 billion. However, analysts have recently become more bearish, indicated by a negative Earnings ESP of -1.10% and a Zacks Rank of #4, making a positive EPS surprise difficult to conclusively predict despite a past beat in the last reported quarter. Investors are advised to consider various factors beyond just the earnings report for this stock.

feed.title: International Paper - Director/PDMR Shareholding - Replacement
feed.summary: International Paper issued a replacement announcement regarding Director/PDMR shareholdings, specifically correcting the number of shares withheld for taxes for Timothy S. Nicholls. The initial announcement on February 4, 2026, incorrectly calculated tax withholdings for Nicholls, an expatriate, due to an administrative error regarding federal taxes. This updated notification restates the transaction details for Nicholls and includes vesting information for W. Thomas Hamic and Joseph R. Saab.

feed.title: Stifel raises Generac stock price target to $235 on data center growth
feed.summary: Stifel has increased its price target for Generac Holdings (NYSE:GNRC) to $235 from $210, maintaining a Buy rating, driven by strong growth prospects in the Commercial & Industrial (C&I) segment, particularly from data center orders. Despite recent Q4 2025 financial results missing analyst expectations (EPS of $1.61 vs. $1.77 forecast, revenue of $1.1 billion vs. $1.16 billion forecast), investor optimism for strategic updates and future growth led to a pre-market stock increase. The revised price target is based on estimated 2027 EBITDA at a 14-15x multiple, contrasting with the current InvestingPro EV/EBITDA multiple of 29.87x which suggests the stock might be overvalued.

feed.title: Justice Department Antitrust Chief Slater Resigns
feed.summary: Abigail Slater, the Justice Department's top antitrust official, resigned on Thursday, raising questions about the future of competition policy in the Trump administration. Her resignation occurs just weeks before a significant trial against Live Nation Entertainment Inc. over its Ticketmaster ticketing arm. Slater announced her departure via a statement on X.

feed.title: Deere (DE) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Deere (DE) is expected to announce its earnings for the quarter ended January 2026, with Wall Street anticipating a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues. Based on a positive Earnings ESP of +0.13% and a Zacks Rank of #3, Deere is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate of $1.92 per share. Investors will be watching the actual results closely to see how they compare to these estimates and what impact they have on the stock price.

feed.title: DA Davidson Adjusts Price Target on Akamai Technologies to $125 From $115, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: DA Davidson has increased its price target for Akamai Technologies (AKAM) to $125 from $115, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other recent analyst activities, including Oppenheimer also raising their price target and Morgan Stanley upgrading their rating on Akamai. The company's stock is showing positive performance with an over 21% change year-to-date.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Smurfit Westrock to $58 From $52, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Smurfit Westrock (NYSE: SW) to $58 from $52, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued analyst confidence in the paper packaging solutions company. Other firms like Truist Securities, Stifel, RBC, BofA, Citigroup, BNP Paribas, and Wells Fargo have also updated their price targets, generally raising them and maintaining positive ratings.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Parker-Hannifin, Goldman Sachs, GE Aerospace, C.H. Robinson Worldwide and Carpenter Technology
feed.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog features five "old economy" stocks – Parker-Hannifin (PH), Goldman Sachs (GS), GE Aerospace (GE), C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW), and Carpenter Technology (CRS) – that posted solid earnings in their latest reported quarters. These companies, all carrying a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), are highlighted for their strong financial performance, strategic initiatives, and potential for continued growth. The article suggests that investing in such historically stable sectors can provide diversification and profits amid an AI-driven market.

feed.title: Ancora Holdings could see multiple ways to win in Warner Bros. fight
feed.summary: Cleveland-based activist investor Ancora Holdings Group is making waves in the Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. takeover fight, advocating for a rejection of Netflix's offer in favor of Paramount Skydance Corp. The firm, known for its strategic activism, seeks "multiple ways to win" in high-profile M&A situations, distinguishing itself through both aggressive public campaigns and behind-the-scenes collaborations. Ancora's activist fund, Bellator, saw a significant gain in 2025, solidifying its reputation as a top-tier activist firm with $11 billion in assets under management.

feed.title: Renaissance Loads Up on Netflix, Lam Research, ServiceNow — Smart Money Signals Shift
feed.summary: Hedge fund Renaissance Group significantly increased its holdings in Netflix, Lam Research, and ServiceNow, signaling a strategic shift towards streaming, AI infrastructure, and enterprise software. Beyond tech, Renaissance also made new investments in healthcare, financial services, and commodities, indicating a broader diversification across sectors. These moves suggest that institutional investors are focusing on high-growth tech enablers and expanding into new opportunities.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an earnings release anticipated on February 19. The company has a positive Earnings ESP of +1.02% and a Zacks Rank of #3, indicating a high probability of beating the consensus EPS estimate. Over the last four quarters, Akamai has consistently surpassed EPS estimates.

feed.title: Here’s Why Analog Devices (ADI) Rose in Q4
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) saw its stock rise in Q4 2025, reaching new all-time highs, according to Carillon Tower Advisers' investor letter. The semiconductor company's robust earnings performance suggests that the analog cycle is past its bottom and poised for an early recovery in 2026. Despite ADI not being among the top 30 most popular stocks for hedge funds, its strong recent performance and positive outlook from investment firms highlight its potential for continued growth.

feed.title: Celebrity Cruises Earns Forbes Travel Guide’s First-Ever Five-Star Rating for a Restaurant at Sea
feed.summary: Celebrity Cruises has achieved a new milestone as Forbes Travel Guide awarded its first-ever Five-Star rating for a restaurant at sea to Chef Daniel Boulud's Le Voyage on Celebrity Xcel. This builds on Celebrity's prior recognition as the first cruise line to earn a Forbes Travel Guide Star Award in 2023. The rating underscores Celebrity Cruises' commitment to elevated hospitality and exceptional culinary experiences.
